extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is violated by transition [t_resume_glitch], which on a send_attempt with guard (opted_out and ticks >= 3) sets sent := true while opted_out is true.

Reachability of the violating state within the 7-event bound:
- ticks only increases via the tick event (capped at 3). Three ticks reach ticks=3 (each tick also sets sent:=false, harmless here). opted_out remains false.
- The optout event sets opted_out:=true, sent:=false, and leaves ticks unchanged at 3.
- A subsequent send_attempt has guards: t_send_allowed (not opted_out)=false; t_send_blocked (opted_out)=true; t_resume_glitch (opted_out and ticks>=3)=true. Both t_send_blocked and t_resume_glitch are enabled. Under the spec's enumeration of enabled transitions, an execution exists in which t_resume_glitch fires, setting sent:=true.

Resulting state: opted_out=true, ticks=3, sent=true. Evaluating opted_out implies not sent = (true implies not true) = false, so the globally invariant fails.

Witness execution (5 events, within the 7-event bound): tick, tick, tick, optout, send_attempt. The t_resume_glitch transition is the intended-bug: it re-enables sending after a tick-based timeout window even though the member has opted out, defeating the opt-out guarantee. Property is violated.
